groups.title: Inflation
groups.blurb: Three ways the U.S. measures inflation — consumer prices (CPI), producer prices (PPI), and the PCE deflator.

indicators.title: US Consumer Price Index
indicators.subtitle: Headline and core CPI, components, and energy prices — sourced live from the U.S. Bureau of Labor Statistics.
indicators.card: Headline & core CPI, monthly change, components, energy prices. Source: BLS.

indicators.title: US Producer Price Index
indicators.subtitle: Final-demand prices producers receive — headline, core, goods, services, foods, energy. Source: BLS.
indicators.card: Final-demand prices producers receive — headline, core, goods, services. Source: BLS.

indicators.title: PCE Price Index
indicators.subtitle: The Fed's preferred inflation gauge — headline, core, services, and supercore. Source: BEA.
indicators.card: The Fed's preferred gauge — headline, core, services, supercore. Source: BEA.

indicators.title: US Labor Market
indicators.subtitle: Payrolls, unemployment, wages, hours, participation, and JOLTS — sourced live from the U.S. Bureau of Labor Statistics.
indicators.card: Payrolls, unemployment, JOLTS, wage growth, participation.

groups.blurb: Retail sales, income & spending, sentiment & confidence.

groups.blurb: Treasury yields, the curve, credit spreads, equities, commodities.
